name: PET Bottle
description:
Resource cycle for PET beverage bottle packaging.

Graph nodes show rolled-up CO₂e (emissions only). Click a node for the step breakdown. The statement table compares Manufacture / Recycle / Reuse / Landfill pathways. Transformation is where ready bottle material becomes the filled article for the consumer.
co2e_basis: Absolute process emissions only (no avoided-burden credits). Illustrative order-of-magnitude from PET packaging LCAs — not a certified EPD. Virgin path ≈ feedstock + resin/blow-mold; recycle ≈ collect + rPET remelt; reuse ≈ return + wash. All routes emit CO₂e; reuse < recycle < virgin manufacture. Beverage contents are out of scope.

nodes.label: Raw Material
nodes.details:
Most PET starts as naphtha or natural-gas liquids.

1. Extract and refine hydrocarbons.
2. Produce PTA and MEG monomers.
3. Polymerize to PET resin pellets; ship to converters.

Bio-based MEG exists but is a small share of bottle resin.
nodes.co2e_note: ~1.1 kg CO₂e/kg for upstream feedstock + monomer route to bottle-grade resin pellets (illustrative).

nodes.label: Manufacture
nodes.details:
1. Injection-mold resin into preforms.
2. Stretch-blow mold into bottles; trim and inspect.
3. Pack empty ware for filling lines.

rPET flake/pellets rejoin preform molding (folded into Recycle → Transformation as remolded ware ready to fill).
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.9 kg CO₂e/kg for preform + stretch-blow + trim of virgin resin bottles.

nodes.label: Recycle
nodes.summary: Collect, wash, flake → rPET remold → rejoin Transformation
nodes.details:
Kerbside / deposit → sort clear PET → wash & flake → solid-state / remelt → preform/blow → empty ware back into Transformation.

Figures are the remanufacture path emissions — not a credit for avoiding virgin resin. Food-grade bottle-to-bottle needs clean clear streams.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.55 kg CO₂e/kg for collect/sort/wash/flake plus remold into bottle ware. Lower than virgin, still positive.

nodes.label: Reuse
nodes.summary: Return, wash, inspect — then back into Transformation
nodes.details:
Deposit–return refillable PET (or durable bottle schemes): reverse logistics → industrial wash → inspect → rejoin Transformation.

Each trip still emits (haul + wash), but far less than remolding or virgin resin.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.10 kg CO₂e/kg per reuse trip (return haul + industrial wash). Still emits, but less than recycle remold.

nodes.label: Transformation
nodes.summary: Turn ready material into its final form for the end user
nodes.details:
Empty bottles from Manufacture, Recycle, or Reuse are filled, capped, labelled, and packed for the consumer.

Beverage contents’ footprint is out of scope for the packaging functional unit. Inbound empties and outbound freight are Deliver edges.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.08 kg CO₂e/kg packaging for fill/cap/label utilities attributed to the bottle (not the drink).

nodes.label: Use
nodes.summary: Consumer drinks; bottle becomes empty again
nodes.details:
Use-phase GHG for the PET object is negligible. The important choice is the next edge:

• Landfill — material lost
• Recycle — rPET remold / rejoin Transformation
• Reuse — wash and rejoin Transformation
nodes.co2e_note: ~0 kg CO₂e for the bottle during use. Climate impact is decided by which outgoing branch is taken.

nodes.label: Landfill
nodes.details: Residual-waste collection and burial, or leakage to litter. PET persists for decades; embodied carbon and material are lost to the packaging loop.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.03 kg CO₂e/kg collection + disposal ops. Climate cost of this route is that the next bottle needs virgin manufacture.

edges.co2e_note: ~0.04 kg CO₂e/kg regional empty-bottle haul to the filler.
~0.06 kg CO₂e/kg filled outbound distribution (excl. beverage cold-chain where out of scope).
